_readme: Pauline Journeys Data — compiled for Paul's World interactive map. All dates AD. Coordinates [longitude, latitude]. Dates are approximate scholarly consensus; see 'dateDebated' flags.

cities.name: Antioch
cities.description: Paul's primary missionary base; the first church to commission cross-cultural mission

cities.name: Seleucia Pieria
cities.description: Port of Antioch; Paul's point of departure for Cyprus on the first journey

cities.name: Tarsus
cities.description: Paul's birthplace and hometown; a center of learning in the Roman world

cities.name: Damascus
cities.description: Site of Paul's conversion encounter; he escaped the city in a basket

cities.name: Jerusalem
cities.description: Center of the early church; Paul's visits mark key turning points including the Jerusalem Council and his final arrest

cities.name: Caesarea
cities.description: Roman administrative capital of Judaea; Paul held prisoner here 2 years under Felix and Festus

cities.name: Salamis
cities.description: Principal port of Cyprus; Paul and Barnabas preached in synagogues here

cities.name: Paphos
cities.description: Capital of Roman Cyprus; Sergius Paulus the proconsul converted here; Elymas the sorcerer struck blind

cities.name: Perga
cities.description: City in Pamphylia; John Mark departed for Jerusalem here on the first journey

cities.name: Attalia
cities.description: Port city; Paul and Barnabas sailed from here back to Antioch to conclude the first journey

cities.name: Pisidian Antioch
cities.description: Site of Paul's synagogue sermon in Acts 13 — a model of his gospel proclamation; expelled by Jewish opposition

cities.name: Iconium
cities.description: Paul preached here with great fruit; faced violent opposition and stoning attempts

cities.name: Lystra
cities.description: Paul healed a lame man; mistaken for Hermes; stoned and left for dead; Timothy's hometown

cities.name: Derbe
cities.description: Southernmost point of the first journey; many disciples made; Paul turned back north from here

cities.name: Troas
cities.description: The Macedonian call vision occurred here; also site of Eutychus raised from the dead; Paul left his cloak here (2 Tim 4:13)

cities.name: Samothrace
cities.description: Island stop on Paul's voyage from Troas to Neapolis

cities.name: Neapolis
cities.description: Paul's landing point in Europe — the first soil of the European continent he stepped onto

cities.name: Philippi
cities.description: First European church — Lydia converted by the river; Paul and Silas imprisoned; jailer baptized at midnight; the church that supported Paul consistently

cities.name: Thessalonica
cities.description: Paul preached 3 Sabbaths; significant converts; Jason's house attacked; Paul received two financial gifts from here while in Thessalonica

cities.name: Berea
cities.description: The Bereans examined the Scriptures daily; many believed including prominent Greek women

cities.name: Athens
cities.description: The Areopagus address — Paul engaged Greek philosophy with the resurrection of Christ; the 'Unknown God' sermon

cities.name: Corinth
cities.description: Paul's 18-month base on the 2nd journey; tent-maker with Aquila and Priscilla; vision assuring him; center of two major letters; Romans written here

cities.name: Cenchreae
cities.description: Eastern port of Corinth; Paul shaved his head here (Nazirite vow); Phoebe was a deacon of the church here (Romans 16:1)

cities.name: Ephesus
cities.description: Paul's 3-year base on the 3rd journey; the great riot of Artemis; Tyrannus hall lectures; Timothy left in charge; one of Paul's most pivotal ministry locations

cities.name: Miletus
cities.description: Paul's farewell address to the Ephesian elders — one of the most moving passages in Acts; he predicted he would not see them again

cities.name: Assos
cities.description: Paul walked overland from Troas to Assos while others sailed; they met there and continued together

cities.name: Mitylene
cities.description: Capital of Lesbos; one-night stop on Paul's final voyage south from Macedonia

cities.name: Chios
cities.description: Island stop on Paul's final voyage from Macedonia toward Jerusalem

cities.name: Samos
cities.description: Island stop on Paul's final voyage; paused at Trogyllium nearby

cities.name: Cos
cities.description: Island stop on Paul's final voyage toward Jerusalem after leaving Miletus

cities.name: Rhodes
cities.description: Major island stop on Paul's voyage; the great Colossus was already gone by Paul's time

cities.name: Patara
cities.description: Paul changed ships here from the island-hopping vessel to a direct ship to Phoenicia

cities.name: Myra
cities.description: The centurion found an Alexandrian grain ship here to transfer Paul and other prisoners to Rome

cities.name: Cnidus
cities.description: The ship struggled against adverse winds here and could not make harbor; forced to sail south to Crete

cities.name: Fair Havens
cities.description: Paul warned against sailing on; the majority voted to try for Phoenix — the decision that led to the storm and shipwreck

cities.name: Malta
cities.description: 3-month stay after shipwreck; Paul bitten by viper unharmed; healed many; extraordinary hospitality from the islanders

cities.name: Syracuse
cities.description: 3-day stop in Sicily on the final leg of the voyage to Rome

cities.name: Rhegium
cities.description: Tip of the Italian boot; 1-day stop waiting for favorable wind to round into the Tyrrhenian Sea toward Puteoli

cities.name: Puteoli
cities.description: Main Roman port for eastern shipping; Paul found believers here and stayed 7 days before the final road to Rome

cities.name: Rome
cities.description: Paul's 2-year house arrest (~AD 60–62) — wrote Philippians, Colossians, Ephesians, Philemon; the center of the empire and Paul's ultimate destination; traditional site of his martyrdom c. AD 67

cities.name: Puteoli
cities.description: Port of Rome; Paul found a community of believers here and stayed 7 days

cities.name: Sidon
cities.description: First port of call on Paul's voyage to Rome; the centurion Julius allowed Paul to visit friends

cities.name: Tyre
cities.description: 7-day stay on Paul's final journey to Jerusalem; disciples through the Spirit urged Paul not to go

cities.name: Ptolemais
cities.description: One day spent with the brothers on Paul's final journey toward Jerusalem

cities.name: Dyrrachium
cities.description: Western end of the Via Egnatia; sea crossing from Brundisium; key waypoint on overland route between Rome and the eastern provinces

cities.name: Brundisium
cities.description: Eastern end of the Via Appia; standard embarkation point for crossing the Adriatic to Dyrrachium and the Via Egnatia east

cities.name: Nicopolis
cities.description: Paul planned to winter here after his first Roman imprisonment; he urged Titus to join him here

cities.name: Crete
cities.description: Paul left Titus here to 'set in order what remains and appoint elders in every town'

cities.name: Colossae
cities.description: Church in the Lycus Valley; Paul probably never visited personally but wrote to them; Epaphras planted the church

cities.name: Laodicea
cities.description: Sister church to Colossae; Paul's letter was to be read there also; later one of the seven churches of Revelation

journeys.name: First Missionary Journey
journeys.description: Sent by the Antioch church; first deliberate Gentile mission into Asia Minor; Cyprus and the southern Galatian cities

waypoints.note: Commissioned by the church at Antioch; set apart by the Holy Spirit
Set sail from Antioch's port
Proclaimed the word in the Jewish synagogues
Elymas the sorcerer blinded; Sergius Paulus the proconsul believed
John Mark departed for Jerusalem; cause of later dispute with Barnabas
Great synagogue sermon; many Gentiles believed; expelled by Jewish opposition
Signs and wonders; divided city; plot to stone them
Lame man healed; called Zeus and Hermes; stoned and left for dead; raised up
Preached and made many disciples; southernmost point of the journey
Return journey; strengthening and appointing elders
Return; elders appointed
Return; elders appointed
Preached the word
Sailed from Attalia back to Antioch
Report to the church; 'God had opened a door of faith to the Gentiles'

journeys.name: Second Missionary Journey
journeys.description: Overland through Galatia to Troas; the Macedonian call brings the gospel to Europe for the first time; 18 months in Corinth

waypoints.note: Departed after the Jerusalem Council; Silas joins Paul
Overland through Syria and Cilicia strengthening the churches
Delivered the Jerusalem Council decrees
Timothy joins the team; circumcised because of the Jews
Churches strengthened in faith and growing in number
Forbidden by the Spirit to speak in Asia; passed through Phrygia and Galatia
The Macedonian vision: 'Come over to Macedonia and help us'; Luke joins
One night stop on the sea crossing
First footfall on European soil
Lydia converted by the river; Paul and Silas imprisoned; earthquake; jailer baptized at midnight
Three Sabbaths in the synagogue; Jason's house attacked; 'turned the world upside down'
Noble Bereans searched the Scriptures daily; many believed including prominent Greek women
Provoked by idols; Areopagus address; a few believed including Dionysius and Damaris
18 months; Aquila and Priscilla; tent-making; vision: 'Do not be afraid'; Gallio refuses to judge
Paul shaved his head — a Nazirite vow; Phoebe is deacon here (Rom 16:1)
Brief visit; left Priscilla and Aquila; promised to return
Greeted the church
Went up and greeted the church
Spent some time there; end of second journey

journeys.name: Third Missionary Journey
journeys.description: Three years in Ephesus as the base; major letter-writing period; final return through Macedonia and Greece; farewell voyage to Jerusalem

waypoints.note: Departed for another journey; 'strengthening all the disciples'
Through Galatia and Phrygia; strengthening the disciples
3 YEARS: Hall of Tyrannus; extraordinary miracles; books burned; riot of Artemis; seven sons of Sceva
Hoped to find Titus; went on to Macedonia
Macedonia ministry; Titus met him here with good news from Corinth; wrote 2 Corinthians
Visited and encouraged the churches of Macedonia
Visited the church
3 months; wrote Romans — the great summary of the gospel; collected offering for Jerusalem
Changed plans due to a Jewish plot; sailed from Philippi after Passover
7 days; Eutychus falls from window and is raised; bread broken
Paul walked overland alone; met the others at Assos
Overnight stop
Sailed past
Stopped at Trogyllium; overnight
Farewell address to Ephesian elders; 'I do not count my life of any value'; wept and embraced
Island stop
Island stop
Changed to a ship sailing directly to Phoenicia
7 days unloading cargo; disciples urged Paul through the Spirit not to go to Jerusalem
One day with the brothers
Philip the Evangelist's house; Agabus bound himself with Paul's belt: 'The Jews will bind the owner of this belt'
Warmly received; presented the Gentile offering; arrested in the temple; the third journey ends here

journeys.name: Journey to Rome
journeys.description: Arrest in Jerusalem; 2-year Caesarean imprisonment; dramatic voyage with storm and shipwreck; 2-year house arrest in Rome

waypoints.note: Arrested in the temple; Claudius Lysias intervened; plot on his life
2-year imprisonment under Felix (waiting for a bribe), then Festus; Paul appeals to Caesar
First port; centurion Julius allowed Paul to visit friends
Transferred to Alexandrian grain ship bound for Italy
Adverse winds; could not make harbor
Paul warned against sailing; overruled; the decision that led to the storm
3-month stay after shipwreck; viper unharmed; Publius's father healed; many others healed
3-day stop in Sicily
1-day stop; south wind; rounded the toe of Italy
Found brothers; stayed 7 days; then the Appian Way to Rome
2-year house arrest; chained to a guard; 'welcomed all who came to him'; wrote Philippians, Colossians, Ephesians, Philemon

journeys.name: Post-Rome Ministry
journeys.description: Traditional account: released ~AD 62; further travel including possible Spain; pastoral work in Ephesus, Macedonia, Crete; second Roman arrest and martyrdom ~AD 67
journeys._note: This journey is reconstructed from the Pastoral Epistles and early church tradition; it cannot be harmonized with Acts and involves scholarly inference

waypoints.note: Released from first imprisonment; 'the boundary of the West' (1 Clement 5:7) suggests travel west, possibly Spain
Left Timothy here to guard against false teaching; Paul moved on
Paul expected to come soon; Timothy was to come to him
Left Titus to 'set in order what remains and appoint elders in every town'
Planned to winter here; urged Titus to join him
Left his cloak and books here (asked Timothy to bring them)
Second arrest; chained in prison 'like a criminal'; deserted by many; only Luke remained
Martyrdom under Nero, traditional date; 'I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race'

books.name: Galatians
books.dateNote: South Galatia/early date (AD 48–49, post-1st journey, pre-Jerusalem Council) vs. North Galatia/late date (AD 53–55, during 3rd journey from Ephesus). Conservative/evangelical scholarship generally favors the earlier date.
books.theme: Justification by faith alone; freedom from the Mosaic law; the curse of legalism

books.name: 1 Thessalonians
books.dateNote: Written from Corinth after Timothy returned with news of the Thessalonian church. One of Paul's earliest surviving letters.
books.theme: The Lord's return; living to please God; comfort for those who have died

books.name: 2 Thessalonians
books.dateNote: Written shortly after 1 Thessalonians from Corinth; corrects misunderstanding about the Day of the Lord and addresses idle behavior.
books.theme: Correcting misunderstanding about the parousia; the man of lawlessness; faithful work while waiting
books.attributionNote: Debated by some scholars on grounds of eschatological emphasis and style; majority conservative view is Pauline.

books.name: 1 Corinthians
books.dateNote: Written from Ephesus during Paul's 3-year stay; responding to a report from Chloe's people and a letter of questions from the Corinthians.
books.theme: Unity in Christ; spiritual gifts; the resurrection; the Lord's Supper; love as the supreme way

books.name: 2 Corinthians
books.writingLocationNote: Written from Macedonia (likely Philippi or Thessalonica) after Titus brought good news of Corinthian repentance
books.dateNote: Written from Macedonia after Paul left Ephesus; Titus had delivered an earlier 'severe letter' and returned with news of reconciliation.
books.theme: Paul's apostolic ministry; suffering and glory; the collection for Jerusalem; the new covenant

books.name: Romans
books.writingLocationNote: Written from Corinth (host: Gaius, Rom 16:23) during Paul's 3-month stay before returning to Jerusalem with the collection
books.dateNote: Widely agreed to be the latest of Paul's undisputed letters; written from Corinth c. AD 56–57. Phoebe of Cenchreae (Rom 16:1) likely carried the letter.
books.carrierNote: Phoebe (deacon of Cenchreae) likely carried Romans to Rome
books.theme: Justification by faith; the righteousness of God; Jew and Gentile united in Christ; the gospel Paul planned to preach in Rome and Spain

books.name: Philippians
books.dateNote: Written during Paul's first Roman imprisonment. Some scholars argue Ephesian or Caesarean origin, but the references to the praetorium (1:13) and Caesar's household (4:22) most naturally fit Rome.
books.carrierNote: Epaphroditus recovered from illness and carried the letter back to Philippi
books.theme: Joy in suffering; the mind of Christ (kenosis); contentment; partnership in the gospel

books.name: Colossians
books.dateNote: Prison epistle; Tychicus and Onesimus likely carried it (Col 4:7–9). Some scholars argue for Ephesian origin, but Rome remains the majority view.
books.theme: The cosmic supremacy of Christ; warning against speculative philosophy and ascetic practice; the new life in Christ
books.attributionNote: Some scholars question Pauline authorship on grounds of vocabulary and Christology; majority conservative and moderate scholarship affirms it.

books.name: Ephesians
books.dateNote: Likely a circular letter; 'in Ephesus' (1:1) absent in the oldest manuscripts suggesting it was intended for multiple Asian churches. Tychicus carried it (Eph 6:21).
books.theme: The church as the body and bride of Christ; spiritual warfare; the mystery of Jews and Gentiles united
books.attributionNote: Vocabulary and style differ from undisputed letters; many evangelical scholars affirm Pauline authorship as a mature, reflective work.

books.name: Philemon
books.dateNote: The shortest Pauline letter; written alongside Colossians and carried by Onesimus (the runaway slave) himself. Philemon lived in Colossae.
books.theme: Christian reconciliation; the social implications of the gospel; Paul's appeal on behalf of Onesimus

books.name: 1 Timothy
books.writingLocationNote: Written from Macedonia; Paul had left Timothy in Ephesus and was traveling
books.dateNote: Post-Rome, following Paul's release from first imprisonment; reflects a situation not recorded in Acts.
books.theme: Church order; guarding sound doctrine; qualifications for elders and deacons; godly character
books.attributionNote: The Pastorals (1 Tim, Titus, 2 Tim) are among the most debated of the 13; vocabulary differs significantly from undisputed letters. Conservative scholarship affirms Pauline authorship; many non-conservative scholars propose pseudonymity or a Pauline school. All 13 are included here per the scope of this project.

books.name: Titus
books.writingLocationNote: Written en route or from Nicopolis where Paul planned to winter; Titus was in Crete
books.dateNote: Written after leaving Titus in Crete; contemporary with 1 Timothy in the post-Rome period.
books.theme: Appointing elders; sound doctrine; the grace of God that produces godly living
books.attributionNote: See 1 Timothy note; same attribution debate applies.

books.name: 2 Timothy
books.dateNote: Paul's final letter, written from his second and final Roman imprisonment. He knows he is about to die. Luke is his only companion.
books.theme: Faithful endurance to the end; guarding the gospel; Paul's personal testimony facing death
books.attributionNote: Despite the Pastoral authorship debate, 2 Timothy is often treated separately as the most personal and arguably most authentically Pauline of the three.

provinces.description: Roman provincial boundaries for the era of Paul's ministry (~AD 46–67)
provinces.era: The klokantech dataset represents roughly AD 117 (Trajan's era). Boundaries are close to Paul's time with minor differences (e.g., Lycia was added to Pamphylia in AD 43; some eastern provinces differ).
provinces.renderingNotes: Render as semi-transparent fills below the journey lines. Use a low-opacity gold or teal fill (opacity 0.06–0.1) so geography is legible without dominating. Province borders at opacity 0.3–0.4, stroke 1px.

mapConfig.note: Wider viewport than Philippians map to show full journey scope from Spain/Rome to Antioch. Adjust scale to include all relevant cities within view.
